rationale: The cited paper is used multiple times as core evidence that advertising spending is typically procyclical and often cut during downturns, including the specific GDP-advertising elasticity benchmark. The citing paper also explicitly says it builds on Deleersnyder et al. (2009)'s advertising-as-expense versus advertising-as-investment distinction to develop its theoretical framing. This citation helps motivate the research gap and hypothesis logic, so it functions as a meaningful input rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study uses Deleersnyder et al. (2009) as a conceptual and empirical foundation on business-cycle advertising behavior, then investigates a new firm-level antecedent (market orientation facets) for advertising decisions during Turkey's 2001 economic collapse.
